brief_justification: Correctly identifies E4 and 2-fold increase, matching ground truth exactly; Goes three levels deep — identifies bottleneck, explains why the proposal fails, and traces second-order effects (E2 becomes new bottleneck after fix); Explicitly flags the model assumption mid-response and distinguishes simple introductory model from rigorous steady-state analysis; Verifies by computing post-modification throughput and checking whether the new rate actually achieves 2x target under both modeling assumptions; Clearly separates 'what the simple model says' from 'what actually happens in steady-state,' identifying the conditions under which the answer would change; Addresses the proposed alternative (E3) with a specific reason for rejection, but does not systematically evaluate E1, E2, or E5 as candidates; Every numerical claim is derivable step-by-step from the response — current throughput, target, fold-change, and new bottleneck all shown explicitly.
